questions.question: What are democratic rights?
questions.correct_answer: Rights that allow citizens to live with freedom, equality and dignity in a democracy
questions.explanation: Democratic rights protect citizens from misuse of power and ensure fair treatment in society.

questions.question: What are rights?
questions.correct_answer: Reasonable claims of individuals recognized by society and protected by law
questions.explanation: Rights help people live with dignity and ensure that they are treated fairly by society and government.

questions.question: What are Fundamental Rights?
questions.correct_answer: Basic rights guaranteed to citizens by the Constitution of India
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights protect individual freedom and are enforceable by courts.

questions.question: Name the six Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution.
questions.correct_answer: Right to Equality, Right to Freedom, Right against Exploitation, Right to Freedom of Religion, Cultural and Educational Rights, and Right to Constitutional Remedies
questions.explanation: These rights ensure justice, liberty and equality for all citizens.

questions.question: What does the Right to Equality ensure?
questions.correct_answer: Equality before law and no discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
questions.explanation: It ensures equal treatment and equal opportunities for all citizens.

questions.question: What freedoms are included in the Right to Freedom?
questions.correct_answer: Freedom of speech and expression, movement, residence, profession, association and peaceful assembly
questions.explanation: These freedoms allow citizens to express opinions and live freely within reasonable limits.

questions.question: What is prohibited under the Right against Exploitation?
questions.correct_answer: Forced labour, human trafficking and child labour
questions.explanation: This right protects weaker sections of society from exploitation.

questions.question: What does the Right to Freedom of Religion guarantee?
questions.correct_answer: Freedom to practice, profess and propagate any religion
questions.explanation: India is a secular state where all religions are treated equally.

questions.question: What are Cultural and Educational Rights?
questions.correct_answer: Rights that protect the language, culture and educational institutions of minorities
questions.explanation: These rights help minority groups preserve their traditions and identity.

questions.question: Why is the Right to Constitutional Remedies important?
questions.correct_answer: It allows citizens to approach courts when their Fundamental Rights are violated
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called this right the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution.

notes.title: Meaning of Rights
notes.content: Rights are reasonable claims of individuals that are recognized by society and protected by law.

notes.title: Importance of Fundamental Rights
notes.content: Fundamental Rights protect individual liberty and ensure equality and justice in a democratic society.

notes.title: Right to Equality
notes.content: This right ensures that all citizens are treated equally before the law and prohibits discrimination.

notes.title: Right to Freedom
notes.content: Citizens enjoy freedoms such as speech, movement, association and profession with reasonable restrictions.

notes.title: Expansion of Rights
notes.content: New rights such as the Right to Information and Right to Education have expanded the scope of democratic rights.

facts.fact: Fundamental Rights are enforceable by the courts if they are violated.
India follows secularism where the state treats all religions equally.
Child labour in hazardous industries is prohibited under the Constitution.
The Right to Constitutional Remedies allows citizens to approach the Supreme Court or High Court for justice.
